w:r.w:t: This document presents a comprehensive on-chain verification of the Degenter Starknet DEX indexer database schema. Each table has been verified by cross-referencing raw Starknet blockchain event data against indexed records. Additionally, missing fields have been identified, analysed, and prioritised with detailed justifications.
trades — DEX swap trade records (Ekubo / AVNU)
stark_message_l2_to_l1 — L2 to L1 cross-chain messages (currently empty)
Priority classification used throughout this document:
Missing field causes data loss, incorrect analytics, or prevents core functionality. Must be added before production.
Improves query performance or enriches analytics but absence does not break core data.
Enhances analytics depth. Add in a future phase after critical and medium items are resolved.
Column exists but is always NULL or meaningless for the table's purpose. Should be dropped.
FULLY VERIFIED — All fields match raw on-chain Ekubo Swapped event exactly

Trade:0 from transaction 0x023e2dff... was verified against raw Ekubo Swapped event data. Pool key components (token0 ETH, token1 USDC, fee, tick_spacing=5982, extension=0x0), swap amounts, token direction, price derivation, and notional USD were all confirmed correct.
The following fields are currently present and verified correct:
trade_key — Composite primary key (tx_hash:event_index). Correct format.
lane — ACCEPTED_ON_L2. Correct Starknet finality status.
block_number, block_hash, block_timestamp — Correct block context.
transaction_hash, transaction_index, source_event_index — Correct event pointers.
trader_address — Wallet that initiated the swap.
amount0_delta, amount1_delta — Raw signed pool deltas. Verified against on-chain mag+sign values.
price_is_decimals_normalized — Boolean flag. Verified true for ETH/USDC pair.
price_source — ekubo_reported_ratio. Verified against sqrt_ratio_after derivation.
bucket_1m — 1-minute time bucket. Present and correct.
metadata — JSONB with enrichment context, price path, pending flags.
pending_enrichment — Boolean flag. Correctly false after enrichment.

Raw Ekubo event emits locker contract (0x199741...). This is how router protocols are identified. Currently router_protocol is NULL because locker is not captured. Storing this fixes null router detection for all Ekubo trades.

liquidity_after is directly in the raw Swapped event (e.g. 0x319d1cd12d647 = 875,755,891,010,119). Critical for slippage modelling, LP analytics, and impermanent loss calculations. Zero additional parsing cost.

sqrt_ratio_after is in every raw Swapped event. More precise than ratio-derived price. Required for exact OHLCV reconstruction without floating-point drift. Should be stored as TEXT to preserve full 256-bit precision.
tick_after (signed integer) is in every raw Swapped event. Tick-based queries are used by all DEX charting libraries. Currently requires expensive log(sqrt_ratio^2) calculation on every query.

Transaction 0x023e2dff has 3 trades (indices 0,1,2) forming one multi-hop swap. Without this flag, wallet analytics cannot distinguish a single-hop trade from one leg of a 3-hop route. Volume would be triple-counted.
Companion to is_multi_hop. Stores position of this trade within the route (0,1,2...). Together with total_hops enables full route reconstruction.
Total number of hops in the multi-hop transaction. Combined with hop_index allows joining all legs of a route.
Derived from params.amount.sign in raw event: 0=exact input, 1=exact output. Classifies the order type. Important for MEV analysis (exact output orders have different slippage profiles).
Protocol fee charged on the swap. Derivable from amount_in * fee_tier. Needed for protocol revenue tracking and accurate cost analysis for traders. Can be computed at index time from fee in pool_id.

Price impact in basis points: difference between sqrt_ratio before and after the swap. Requires either storing sqrt_ratio_before or joining pool_latest. Critical for MEV detection (high impact = potential sandwich attack).
params.skip_ahead from raw event. Non-zero indicates the swap skipped tick ranges (Ekubo optimisation). Useful for detecting large swaps that traverse multiple tick boundaries.
Enum: token0_for_token1 or token1_for_token0. Derivable from amount signs but having it explicit avoids recalculation on every query. Useful for directional flow analytics.
PARTIALLY VERIFIED — Ekubo-sourced ticks correct. AVNU multi-hop ticks show contamination bugs.

14 price_ticks from transaction 0x00ce1f54... were verified. Ekubo-direct ticks (event indices 4, 33, 40, 44) are correct. AVNU aggregator ticks (event indices 10, 52) show price contamination from multi-hop intermediate amounts — price_usd values of 984 billion and 1000 trillion are clearly erroneous.
tick_key — Composite primary key. Correct format.
lane, block_number, block_hash, block_timestamp — Correct block context.
transaction_hash, transaction_index, source_event_index — Correct event pointers.
source_pool_id — Pool used to derive the price.
quote_token_address — Token used as price denominator.
price_quote — Price in quote token units. Verified for Ekubo ticks. Bug in AVNU multi-hop ticks.
price_usd — USD equivalent. Correct for Ekubo and stable-seeded ticks. Bug in AVNU multi-hop.
price_updated_at_block — Last block where price was updated. Present.
metadata — JSONB with path_source, price_source, execution_protocol.

The raw on-chain sell amount from the AVNU Swap event. Without this, price_quote cannot be independently audited. The ~0.23% systematic price deviation detected in price table would be visible here too. Audit trail requires raw amounts.
Raw on-chain buy amount. Together with sell_amount_raw allows direct computation of price_raw_execution = buy_amount / sell_amount for verification against stored price_quote.

Price computed directly from sell_amount_raw / buy_amount_raw before any enrichment pipeline. Store alongside price_quote so anomalies are detectable. The AVNU multi-hop contamination bug would be caught by comparing these two values.

Integer: how many pool hops separate this price from a known stable coin. 0=direct stable pair, 1=one hop away, 2=two hops. Root cause of AVNU multi-hop contamination bug. Prices with hops_from_stable > 1 should be flagged or discarded from OHLCV.

Boolean: true when source_pool_id starts with avnu: or other aggregator prefix. Single column that separates clean AMM-native prices from aggregator-routed prices. Essential for filtering reliable prices in OHLCV construction.

Precomputed: abs(price_quote - price_raw_execution) / price_raw_execution * 100. The 0.23% AVNU systematic offset and the multi-hop contamination cases (984 billion USD) would both be caught automatically. Flag anything > 1% for review.
For AVNU swaps, the actual token sold by the taker. Currently only token_address and quote_token_address stored, losing directionality for multi-hop aggregated swaps.
For AVNU swaps, the actual token received by the taker. Companion to sell_token_address.

Enum: high / medium / low. Based on: is the pool a direct stable pair? is price fresh? is it aggregator-derived? Binary price_is_stale flag does not capture confidence degradation from multi-hop derivation.
Currently buried inside source_pool_id string. Standalone INTEGER column avoids string parsing on every analytical query that needs fee-tier grouping.
Same problem as tick_spacing. Raw fee value buried in pool_id string. Should be decoded to basis points as a numeric column.
Precomputed: block_number - price_updated_at_block. Both values stored separately but their difference must be calculated on every query. Precomputing saves CPU across millions of tick rows.
For AVNU ticks, the route identifier from the aggregator event. Groups all ticks from the same aggregated swap for correct multi-hop attribution.
For AVNU rows, the underlying execution protocol (ekubo, jediswap, etc.) is in metadata but not top-level. Needed to separate AMM source from aggregator wrapper.
VERIFIED WITH ONE SYSTEMATIC CAVEAT — ~0.23% price deviation from raw on-chain amounts detected across all AVNU-derived rows.

Transaction 0x0201a9... AVNU swap: STRK sold (0x346f9790ef131c00 = 3.7698 STRK), USDT received (0x01fad7 = 129,751 = 0.129751 USDT). Derived execution price = 0.034418 USDT/STRK. Indexer stores 0.034340 — a systematic 0.23% offset consistent across both rows, indicating a computation path difference in the enrichment pipeline rather than a random error.
block_number, block_hash, block_timestamp — Correct block context.
source_pool_id — Pool or aggregator route used. Correct.
price_quote — Enriched price. ~0.23% systematic offset vs raw for AVNU rows.
price_usd — USD equivalent. Logic correct (USDT row = $1 seed, STRK row = price_quote since USDT ~ $1).
metadata — JSONB with pool_id, path_source, price_source, execution_protocol.
NOTE: prices table does NOT have bucket_1m unlike trades and price_ticks. This is a significant omission.

Time-series bucket is present in trades and price_ticks but absent from prices. Without it, joining prices to OHLCV candles requires complex timestamp arithmetic. date_trunc('minute', block_timestamp) — should be added as generated/stored column.
Raw token amount sold in the on-chain event. Required for independent price verification. The detected 0.23% systematic offset can only be investigated if raw amounts are stored.
Raw token amount received in the on-chain event. Together with sell_amount_raw enables direct audit of price_quote accuracy.
Price = buy_amount_human / sell_amount_human computed from raw on-chain amounts. Store as separate column alongside enriched price_quote for audit trail and anomaly detection.
abs(price_quote - price_raw_execution) / price_raw_execution * 100. The 0.23% AVNU offset would be detected automatically. Any row with > 1% deviation should be flagged.
The AVNU Swap event includes taker_address. Not stored in prices table. Useful for detecting wash trading and price manipulation by correlating which wallets trigger which price updates.
Consistent with price_ticks recommendation. Direct stable pair = 0, one-hop = 1, etc. Enables reliable filtering of noisy multi-hop derived prices.
Boolean: source_pool_id starts with avnu: or similar. Separates aggregator prices from AMM-native prices for downstream analytical consumers.
Boolean: is the quote_token a known stable (USDC, USDT, DAI). Trivially derivable from a stablecoins registry. Makes USD-denominated price queries instant without registry join.
block_number - price_updated_at_block. Both columns exist but are never precomputed. Filtering fresh prices (WHERE lag < 10) requires subtraction on every row.
Decimal precision of sell token. Required to interpret sell_amount_raw correctly without joining token registry.
Decimal precision of buy token. Required to interpret buy_amount_raw correctly.
FULLY VERIFIED — Row 2 (source_event_index 29) verified exactly against raw Ekubo Swapped event.

Token pair (0x124aeb.../0x452810...), tick_spacing=354892 (0x56a4c), extension=0x43e4f09c..., fee=0x0 all confirmed correct. liquidity_after = 0x64d9417efb4f42048e8 = 29,765,269,452,296,053,344,488 matches indexer exactly. sqrt_ratio_after = 0x36f2bbeecd16de0ae3105cd7753b883c = 73,038,659,342,521,518,627,787,165,021,708,716,092 matches indexer exactly. Price derived from Q128.128 sqrt_ratio confirmed correct.

IMPORTANT BUG: price_is_decimals_normalized is inconsistent — rows 1-3 have false, row 4 has true. Prices in rows 1-3 are NOT comparable to row 4 without decimal adjustment. This will cause silent errors in any cross-pool analytics.
reserve0, reserve1 — ALWAYS NULL. Ekubo does not emit reserves in events.
liquidity — Verified exact match to raw liquidity_after field in event.
sqrt_ratio — Verified exact match to raw sqrt_ratio_after field in event.
price_token1_per_token0, price_token0_per_token1 — Derived from sqrt_ratio. Correct (tiny rounding from integer precision).
tvl_usd — ALWAYS NULL. Schema field exists but never populated.
metadata — JSONB with protocol, snapshot_kind, receipt_context_id.

Directly in raw Swapped event (signed integer). You store sqrt_ratio but not tick_after. tick_after is required for concentrated liquidity range queries (WHERE tick BETWEEN lower AND upper), fee tier analysis, and detecting tick crossings. Computing tick from sqrt_ratio requires log() on every query — expensive at scale.
Currently only in pool_id string. Must be extracted via string parsing on every query that groups pools by fee tier. Standalone INTEGER column eliminates this overhead.
Fee tier value buried in pool_id string. Should be decoded to a human-readable basis points value (e.g. 100 = 0.01%, 3000 = 0.3%). Critical for pool-level analytics and UI display.

Ekubo extension contract address buried in pool_id string. Extensions implement oracle pools, full-range pools, and other custom logic. Protocol-level analytics require knowing which extension each pool uses.
Raw event emits locker (0x199741822...) which identifies which router/protocol triggered the state change. Not stored. Required to attribute pool state changes to specific protocols.

Swap amounts that caused this pool state snapshot are not stored. You have liquidity and sqrt_ratio after, but not what was traded. TVL change tracking requires joining back to trades table — direct storage makes it instant.
See amount0_delta. Both deltas together with pre/post sqrt_ratio give complete state transition information without additional joins.
Required to fix the price_is_decimals_normalized inconsistency. Store actual decimal values so all consumers can normalise prices themselves regardless of the flag.
params.skip_ahead = 100 in the verified event. Non-zero indicates the swap skipped tick ranges. Relevant for price impact estimation and detecting large swaps.
Per-swap volume through this pool. Not currently stored. Required for computing per-pool volume time series without joining trades table.
Column exists in schema but is never populated across all observed rows. Either implement population logic or drop the column — always-null columns waste storage and mislead consumers.
Ekubo does not emit reserves in swap events. Reserves can be approximated from liquidity + sqrt_ratio + tick range but this is not implemented. Drop or populate properly.
Boolean: did this swap cross a tick boundary? Derivable by comparing tick_after to previous pool snapshot tick. Important for LP impermanent loss analytics.
Price impact in basis points. Approximable from tick delta * tick_spacing once tick_after is stored.
MOSTLY VERIFIED — 4 of 5 rows correct. Row 1 (DAI/USDC) has real volume1 mismatch bug. Multi-trade candle (Row 5) correct.

Verified against raw Ekubo Swapped event for DAI/USDC pool (tick_spacing=200). Pool key, fee, tokens all confirmed. Price derivation via Q128.128 sqrt_ratio formula confirmed: open/high/low/close = 1.000514 correct for DAI/USDC.

BUG DETECTED — Row 1 (trade_count=1, cache_mode=incremental_new): volume1 = 11,978,828 but raw on-chain amount1 = 11,992,606. Difference of 13,778 USDC on a single-trade candle with no prior data is a real bug. Either wrong trade's volume was added or volume1 is populated from a different source than amount1_delta.

Row 5 ETH/USDC aggregated candle (trade_count=5, previous_trade_count=3+2): high > close > open > low ordering valid. volume_usd = $603.52 reasonable for 5 ETH swaps. cache_mode=incremental_append correct.
lane — ACCEPTED_ON_L2. Same on every row — wasted storage.
block_number, block_hash — From the last trade in bucket. Inconsistent semantics for multi-trade candles.
transaction_hash, transaction_index, source_event_index — Only meaningful for single-trade candles. Meaningless for multi-trade.
open, high, low, close — OHLC prices. Verified correct including decimal normalisation.
price_is_decimals_normalized — Boolean. Correct (true for all verified rows).
volume0, volume1 — Token volumes. volume1 BUG detected on Row 1.
volume_usd — Combined USD volume. Reasonable but not per-token attributable.
metadata — JSONB with source, cache_mode, pending_enrichment, previous_trade_count, reconciliation_triggered.

tick_after from the first trade in the bucket. Stored in raw Swapped event. Tick-based charting is used by all major DEX frontends (Uniswap-style). Currently every price chart query must recompute tick from log(sqrt_ratio^2) — expensive at millions of candles.
tick_after from the last trade in the bucket. Companion to tick_open. Together they bound the price range traversed during the minute.

Exact Q128.128 sqrt_ratio value at bucket open. price_is_decimals_normalized=true exists but underlying sqrt_ratio is not stored. Reconstructing it from OHLC prices loses precision due to floating-point arithmetic.
Exact Q128.128 sqrt_ratio value at bucket close. Required for zero-loss price reconstruction and for connecting consecutive candles without drift.
Fee tier in basis points, currently buried in pool_id string. Most common OHLCV query: show me ETH/USDC 0.05% pool candles. Without this column every such query requires string parsing on pool_id.
Same issue as fee_tier_bps. Pool grouping by tick_spacing requires string parsing on every query. Standalone INTEGER column.

USD value of volume0 (token0 side). Currently only combined volume_usd exists. Token-specific USD volume is required for token-level analytics and cannot be reliably derived post-hoc without price at exact trade time.
USD value of volume1 (token1 side). Same reason as volume0_usd.

Volume Weighted Average Price = sum(price * volume) / sum(volume) across all trades in the bucket. Standard professional trading metric. Computing it at query time across millions of candles is prohibitively expensive — must be stored at write time.

liquidity_after from first trade in bucket (e.g. 0xaef30dbfdd90369 = 796,286,350,131,179,369). Liquidity context at candle formation is critical for LP position analytics and impermanent loss calculations.
liquidity_after from last trade in bucket. Together with liquidity_open shows how pool depth changed during the minute.
(high - low) / low * 100. Precomputed volatility indicator. Computing across millions of candles at query time is expensive. Store at write time.
Boolean: trade_count = 1. Single-trade candles have open=high=low=close and are less reliable for OHLCV analysis. Most charting libraries want to filter or display them differently.
Close price of the preceding bucket. seeded_from_previous_close field exists but the actual price value is not stored. Required for gap analysis — detecting price gaps between consecutive minutes.
OHLCV is a time-bucket aggregate, not tied to a specific block. block_hash is meaningless for multi-trade candles. Wastes storage across millions of rows.
Only meaningful for the first trade in a bucket. Meaningless for multi-trade candles. Point back to trades table via pool_id + bucket_start join instead.
Always ACCEPTED_ON_L2 on every single row. Completely uniform — zero analytical value. Significant wasted storage at millions of candle rows.
PIPELINE BUG — Table returned empty for tested signature. Token data exists in system (UI shows Cash token $1.00, 373 holders) but indexer fails to link it.

Response: token_count=0, note='No related token addresses found from signature in trade/price/pool/ohlcv tables.' The pipeline only populates token_metadata when a token appears in a trade/price/pool event. Tokens not yet traded (or with all-failed enrichments) never appear. The table needs an independent seed process from a known token registry.
UI fields verified from screenshot: Cash token, $1.00 price (stable), verified badge, Starknet TVL $313.94K, Cross-Chain Market Cap $311.46K, 373 holders, Token Supply 311.89K.

UI shows 373 holders. This field is absent from schema entirely. Requires indexing Transfer events to track unique addresses with balance > 0. Alternatively, maintain a token_holders table and compute count via materialized view refreshed per block.

UI shows Cross-Chain Market Cap $311.46K. Cross-chain market cap requires external API (CoinGecko/DefiLlama). Store as a column updated by a periodic enrichment job. Starknet-specific TVL should be separate from cross-chain market cap.

UI shows Token Supply on Starknet 311.89K. Different from total_supply (which is cross-chain). Starknet circulating supply = sum of balances on Starknet. Requires Transfer event indexing or periodic RPC call to totalSupply() on the token contract.
Total supply across all chains. For cross-chain tokens (e.g. STRK bridged from Ethereum) this differs from Starknet circulating supply. Required for FDV calculation.
May exist in schema but not verified as populated. Core field — every token needs a USD price. Should be populated by enrichment pipeline from price_ticks or external stable price feed.

Starknet TVL: sum of token reserves across all active pools. UI shows $313.94K. Should be computed from pool_latest and refreshed periodically. If field exists but is NULL, implement population logic.
UI shows a verified badge. Boolean flag. Should be set by manual curation or by presence in starknet-addresses-mainnet registry (same source used by stark_transfers verification_level).
Standard UI metric. Requires price history — compare current price_usd to price 24 hours ago from price_ticks. Update via periodic job.
24-hour rolling volume. Derivable from trades table but expensive to compute on every request. Pre-aggregate and store, refresh hourly.
External identifier for cross-chain price and market cap lookups. Required to populate market_cap_usd and cross-chain total_supply.
Transaction hash of token contract deployment. Useful for token launch analytics and distinguishing old vs new tokens.
Token logo URL for UI display. Can be sourced from CoinGecko or hosted in BeencoLabs CDN.
FULLY VERIFIED — All 6 rows match raw ERC-20 Transfer events exactly. Transfer flow reconstruction correct.

Transaction 0x04ff327b... contains 6 NSTR (Nostra token) transfers forming a complete Nostra lending protocol interaction. source_event_index 25 verified: from=0x4270219d (Router), to=0x191e94b (Trader), value=0x010924008c3cee692f=19,105,396,121,530,165,551. All fields match exactly.

Transfer flow reconstructed: Nostra Pool -> Ekubo Locker -> Router -> Trader -> Router -> Ekubo Locker -> Nostra Pool. Amount difference between outgoing and return leg: 164,962,139,912,495 NSTR = swap fee retained by pool. Correct behavior confirmed.
from_address, to_address — Correct. Verified against raw event.
amount — Raw integer amount. Verified exact match.

metadata — JSONB containing: selector, standard, token_name (Nostra), token_symbol (NSTR), token_decimals (18), amount_encoding (u256), verification_gate, verification_level (static_verified), verification_source.

amount = 19,105,396,121,530,165,551 raw (18 decimals). amount_human = amount / 1e18 = 19.105 NSTR. Without this computed column every query must manually calculate decimals. Frontend and analytics queries both need human-readable amounts.

USD value of this transfer at the time of the block. Without USD values, volume transferred queries are meaningless. Computed as amount_human * price_usd_at_block, joinable from price_ticks by (token_address, block_number).

token_symbol = NSTR buried in metadata JSONB. Top-level column needed for: (1) proper B-tree index, (2) readable query results without ->>'token_symbol' on every select, (3) foreign key join to tokens table.
token_decimals = 18 buried in metadata JSONB. Required to compute amount_human. Every query that needs decimal-adjusted amounts must extract from JSON — inefficient and not indexable.
token_name = Nostra buried in metadata. Human-readable token name needed for display queries without metadata extraction.

No classification of what type of transfer this is. In this 6-transfer transaction: events 20,24,30,35 are internal_routing hops; event 25 is swap_output (DEX -> user); event 29 is swap_input (user -> DEX). Without this, wallet activity analytics are impossible — all 6 transfers appear identical.

Boolean: is this transfer a contract-to-contract internal routing hop (events 20,24,30,35) vs actual user-facing transfer (events 25,29)? Without filtering internal hops, DEX volume is inflated 3-4x because every swap generates multiple transfers.
Classification of from_address and to_address: wallet, dex_pool, router, lending, bridge, unknown. Required for wallet activity analytics to determine if a user sent to a DEX vs another wallet.
block_number is stored but not block_timestamp. Time-based queries (last 24 hours of transfers) require joining blocks table to get timestamps. Direct column eliminates this join.
1-minute time bucket. Present in trades and ohlcv but absent in transfers. Transfer volume over time queries need this for consistency with other tables.
1-hour time bucket. For hourly transfer volume aggregation.
verification_level = static_verified buried in metadata. Top-level column needed to filter unverified token transfers separately from verified ones.

If this transfer is part of a DEX swap, store the trades.trade_key FK directly. Currently can only join via transaction_hash which is ambiguous (multiple trades per tx). Direct FK enables instant trade-to-transfer correlation.
ERC-20 function selector (0x0099cd8b... = transferFrom). Buried in metadata. Top-level column enables protocol-level analytics: how many transfers were transferFrom vs transfer.

Boolean: is this transfer a fee collection? In this transaction, the 164,962 NSTR difference between event 25 and event 29 amounts represents the fee. Detecting fee transfers enables protocol revenue analytics.
CURRENTLY EMPTY — Low priority for Degenter DEX analytics phase.

This table tracks token holder distribution metrics: top-N holder percentages, whale counts, Gini coefficient, Herfindahl index. It is empty because the indexer processes Swap events, not Transfer events. Populating this table requires:
Indexing all ERC-20 Transfer events for tracked tokens
Maintaining a token_holders balance table (address, holder_address, balance_raw)
Running periodic concentration computations (Gini, top-N percentages)
Recommendation: Defer to Phase 3. For current DEX analytics use case, this data is available from CoinGecko / Starkscan API and can be cached rather than computed independently.

If implemented, key fields to add: token_address, block_number, block_timestamp, holder_count, top1_pct, top10_pct, top100_pct, whale_count, whale_pct_combined, herfindahl_index, gini_coefficient, snapshot_kind.
CURRENTLY EMPTY — Only populated by bridge withdrawals and cross-chain governance.

L2-to-L1 messages are emitted by Starknet contracts calling the send_message_to_l1 syscall. Primary use case is StarkGate bridge withdrawals. The table is empty because the indexer currently listens to DEX contract events (Ekubo, AVNU), not Starknet core contract events (address 0x1).

To populate: listen to MessageSentToL1 events from contract 0x0000...0001 (Starknet core). Parse from_address (L2 sender), to_address (L1 recipient), payload array. For StarkGate bridge messages, decode payload[0]=l1_recipient, payload[1..2]=amount (uint256 low/high bits).

Key fields to add: message_hash, from_address, to_address, payload (JSONB), status (SENT/CONSUMED/CANCELLED), consumed_at_block, consumed_at_tx, is_bridge_withdrawal, bridge_token_address, bridge_l1_recipient, bridge_amount_raw, bridge_amount_usd, bucket_1m.

A centralised token registry table is recommended as a foundation for all other tables. Multiple tables currently embed token information redundantly (symbol, decimals, name in stark_transfers metadata; decimals needed in trades, price_ticks, prices; verification status in multiple places).
symbol TEXT NOT NULL, -- ETH, USDC, STRK
name TEXT NOT NULL, -- Ether, USD Coin
INSERT INTO public.tokens (address, symbol, name, decimals, is_stable, is_verified) VALUES
LEFT JOIN tokens t_in ON t_in.address = t.token_in_address
LEFT JOIN tokens t_out ON t_out.address = t.token_out_address;
All fields match raw Ekubo event. locker/liquidity/sqrt_ratio/tick not stored.
Ekubo ticks correct. AVNU multi-hop ticks show contaminated prices (up to 984 billion USD).
Systematic 0.23% AVNU price offset vs raw on-chain amounts. Consistent across both rows.
liquidity and sqrt_ratio exact match. price_is_decimals_normalized inconsistent across rows.
Row 1 DAI/USDC volume1 bug: 11,978,828 vs expected 11,992,606 (delta 13,778 USDC).
Empty for test signature. Event-driven population fails for tokens without recent swaps.
All 6 rows exact match. Transfer flow correctly reconstructed including fee detection.
Requires Transfer event indexing. Low priority for current DEX analytics phase.
Requires Starknet core contract event listening. Populate for bridge withdrawal tracking.
Create tokens registry table and seed with known Starknet tokens
trades: Add locker_address, liquidity_after, sqrt_ratio_after, tick_after, is_multi_hop, hop_index, total_hops
price_ticks: Add hops_from_stable, is_aggregator_derived, sell/buy_amount_raw, price_raw_execution, price_deviation_pct
prices: Add bucket_1m, sell/buy_amount_raw, price_raw_execution, price_deviation_pct
pool_latest: Add tick_after, tick_spacing, fee_tier, extension_address, locker_address, amount0/1_delta. Drop reserve0/1 and tvl_usd (always NULL)
ohlcv_1m: Add tick_open/close, sqrt_ratio_open/close, fee_tier_bps, tick_spacing, volume0/1_usd, vwap. Drop lane, block_hash, transaction_hash
stark_transfers: Add amount_human, amount_usd, token_symbol/decimals/name (top-level), transfer_type, is_internal, counterparty_type
Fix ohlcv_1m volume1 bug — investigate why single-trade candle shows 13,778 USDC discrepancy
Fix AVNU multi-hop price contamination — implement hops_from_stable filter in price derivation pipeline
price_ticks: Add tick_spacing, fee_tier, price_confidence, price_updated_lag_blocks
prices: Add taker_address, is_aggregator_derived, quote_token_is_stable, sell/buy_token_decimals
stark_transfers: Add block_timestamp, bucket_1m/1h/1d, verification_level, related_trade_key
token_metadata: Add price_change_24h_pct, volume_24h_usd, is_verified, coingecko_id. Fix empty-table pipeline bug
Implement Transfer event indexing -> token_holders table -> stark_token_concentration
Implement L2-to-L1 message indexing -> stark_message_l2_to_l1 for bridge withdrawal tracking
ohlcv_1m: Add liquidity_open/close, price_range_pct, is_single_trade, previous_close
Degenter Indexer Schema Report | BeencoLabs | April 2026
Verification based on live Starknet mainnet block data. All hex values cross-referenced against raw on-chain events.
